applicant_information.minimum_salary_expected: $55,000 per year (base salary), open to discussion depending on overall benefits and responsibilities

education.college_major: B.S. in Business Administration, Concentration in Human Resources Management
education.job_related_skills:
SHRM-CP certified (earned 2023); strong working knowledge of California and federal employment laws including FMLA/CFRA, ADA/FEHA, wage and hour, and meal/rest break compliance. Hands-on experience processing payroll for 80–120 employees using ADP Workforce Now, including new hire setup, terminations, garnishments, and auditing timecards for accuracy.

Three years of experience in a multi-location environment supporting managers with full-cycle recruitment (posting, screening, interviewing, reference checks, and offer letters) for administrative, customer service, and light industrial roles. Comfortable writing detailed job descriptions and tailoring interview questions to competencies.

Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUP, pivot tables, conditional formatting) for HR reporting and headcount/turnover analysis; experienced with HRIS data entry, reporting, and maintaining confidential personnel files both electronically and in locked physical storage.

Conflict resolution and employee relations: have coached supervisors on delivering corrective action, documented verbal and written warnings, and participated in investigations related to attendance issues and team conflicts. I make a point of listening to all sides, documenting dates and facts carefully, and escalating appropriately when patterns emerge.

Bilingual in English and conversational Vietnamese; frequently assisted Vietnamese-speaking employees in understanding onboarding paperwork, benefits enrollment, and safety policies, ensuring they had access to information in their preferred language.

Coordinated onboarding and orientation for new hires: prepared paperwork packets, verified I-9 documentation using E-Verify, scheduled drug screens and background checks through third-party vendors, and conducted first-day orientation covering company policies, safety procedures, and benefits overview.

No formal military service, but I have volunteered with a local veterans’ employment nonprofit, helping review resumes and prepare candidates for interviews, which has strengthened my ability to translate specialized experience into job-relevant language.

work_availability.special_work_schedule_requests:
Prefer a standard Monday–Friday daytime schedule (approximately 8:00 AM–5:00 PM with a 1-hour lunch). I am flexible to adjust start and end times slightly (e.g., 7:30 AM–4:30 PM or 8:30 AM–5:30 PM) based on team needs.

I am generally available for occasional overtime during peak periods such as year-end, open enrollment, or major HR initiatives (policy rollouts, system implementation, etc.). I can also attend early-morning or early-evening calls with vendors or remote locations with advance notice.

I currently have a weekly family obligation on Wednesday evenings after 6:30 PM, so I try to avoid late meetings on that specific evening when possible, but I can make exceptions for critical, time-sensitive issues if given notice.
